metadata.title: States PCS: Maharashtra GK Questions for MPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which cave complex in Maharashtra has India’s oldest wall paintings?
questions.explanation: Ajanta Caves in Maharashtra, dating back to the 2nd century BC, have India’s oldest surviving wall paintings depicting Buddhist themes, preceding other famous rock-cut sites like Ellora. These murals are considered masterpieces of ancient Indian art, illustrating Jataka tales and other religious stories.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Bhillama I the Great was a prominent ruler of the Seuna (Yadava) dynasty. The Sangamner records document his achievements. The Yadava dynasty later established Devagiri (modern Daulatabad) as its capital. Bhillama I actively resisted the Rashtrakutas in Maharashtra. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Bhillama I was a prominent ruler of the Seuna (Yadava) dynasty. Statement 2 is incorrect: There is no historical evidence that the Sangamner records document his achievements. Statement 3 is correct: The Yadavas established Devagiri as their capital. Statement 4 is correct: Bhillama I resisted the Rashtrakutas. Thus, only statements 1, 3, and 4 are correct.

questions.question: Who founded the Rescue Foundation in Maharashtra in 1993?
questions.explanation: The Rescue Foundation, an anti-trafficking NGO based in Mumbai, was founded in 1993 by Balkrishna Acharya. It works towards rescuing, rehabilitating, and repatriating victims of human trafficking, especially women and children, across Maharashtra and India.

questions.question: The Dholya Ganapati Temple, an important pilgrimage site in Wai, Maharashtra, is located on the banks of which river?
questions.explanation: Dholya Ganapati Temple in Wai, Maharashtra, sits on the banks of the Krishna River. Wai is known as Dakshin Kashi due to its numerous temples, with Dholya Ganapati attracting devotees for its large Ganesh idol and spiritual significance.

questions.question: Matoba Dam is built on which river in Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Matoba Dam is an earthfill dam on the Mulamutha River near Daund, Maharashtra, with a height of 17.5 meters, length of 1,662 meters, and a gross storage capacity of 45,200 cubic kilometers.

questions.question: Who established the Holkar dynasty of Malwa?
questions.explanation: Malhar Rao Holkar (1693–1766) was the founder of the Holkar dynasty in Malwa. Appointed by Maratha Peshwa Bajirao I, he established his rule in Indore and expanded Maratha influence across north-central India. His leadership helped establish Holkars as prominent Malwa rulers.

questions.question: With which river does Wardha merge to form the Pranhita River?
questions.explanation: Wardha River joins Wainganga River at the Maharashtra border, forming the Pranhita River, a major tributary of the Godavari. Both rivers flow predominantly in Maharashtra, and their confluence is significant for regional hydrology and water management.

questions.question: Who was the Mughal emperor during the Deccan famine in Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: The Deccan famine of 1630–1632 occurred during Shah Jahan’s reign, affecting Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Deccan regions. Around 7.4 million people died. Causes included drought, crop failures, locust attacks, and administrative diversion of food to armies. Relief measures were largely ineffective.

questions.question: Which turmeric variety from Wardha received Maharashtra’s first GI tag?
questions.explanation: Waigaon Turmeric from Wardha, Maharashtra, received a GI tag in 2016, making it the first turmeric variety from the state to achieve this status. The GI tag recognizes its unique quality and geographical origin.

questions.question: With which organization was Keshavrao Vichare associated?
questions.explanation: Keshavrao Vichare was elected President and General President of the Satyashodhak Samaj and founded the Satyashodhak Vidyarthi Sangh. The Satyashodhak Samaj was established by Jyotirao Phule in Maharashtra to promote social reform and equality among castes.
